name: Selborne
overview: Selborne is a historic village in East Hampshire, about 3.9 miles south of Alton and just within the northern edge of the South Downs National Park, with a population of around 1,288 (2011 census, including Oakhanger). It is famous as the home of the 18th-century naturalist Revd Gilbert White, whose 'The Natural History of Selborne' (1789) has never been out of print. The Grade I listed church of St Mary the Virgin dates from the late 12th century, and Gilbert White's House (The Wakes), with the Oates Museum, draws visitors from across the country.
character: A picturesque, nationally significant heritage village beneath the wooded Selborne Hanger, quiet and rural with a strong literary and natural-history pull.
amenities: The Selborne Arms (village pub) and the Jubilee Tap bar
Village shop and Post Office (with bookshop)
Gilbert White's House, gardens, cafe and farm shop
St Mary the Virgin Church (Grade I listed)

commutes.notes: ~3.9 miles via B3006; Alton has the nearest mainline station
Via Alton, Petersfield or Liss stations
Number 38 bus runs through the village (limited service)

prices.summary: A sought-after heritage village in the South Downs National Park; period cottages and character homes command a premium, with limited supply. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Gilbert White's House, gardens and the Oates Museum
The Zig-Zag Path up Selborne Hanger and circular walks
Selborne Common and Lythes (National Trust land)
St Mary the Virgin Church with Gilbert White windows
Walking in the South Downs National Park
placesToEat: White's Cafe at Gilbert White's House
pros: Outstanding South Downs setting with National Trust land on the doorstep
Rich heritage and visitor attractions (Gilbert White's House)
Village pub, shop and primary school retained
cons: No railway station; rely on Alton/Petersfield and limited buses
Visitor traffic to Gilbert White's House in season
Limited everyday amenities beyond the basics
whoItSuits: Buyers drawn to a beautiful, heritage-rich South Downs village who value walking and nature over urban convenience.

sources.label: Gilbert White's House & Gardens - Visit Hampshire
